content: Diagnostic approach: First confirm hypercortisolism with one of four tests (all have false positives/negatives): (1) Late-night salivary cortisol (×2) - elevated; (2) 24-hour urinary free cortisol (×2-3) - elevated; (3) 1mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test - morning cortisol >1.8 mcg/dL indicates abnormal suppression; (4) Midnight serum cortisol - elevated. If confirmed, measure plasma ACTH to determine ACTH-dependence: suppressed (<5 pg/mL) indicates ACTH-independent (adrenal source); normal/elevated indicates ACTH-dependent. For ACTH-dependent: high-dose dexamethasone suppression test (Cushing disease usually suppresses, ectopic doesn't) and/or CRH stimulation test; pituitary MRI; if discordant, inferior petrosal sinus sampling (gold standard to distinguish pituitary vs ectopic). For ACTH-independent: adrenal CT/MRI. Screen for complications: bone density, glucose, lipids, blood pressure.
